questions.question: Approximate $\int_{1}^{3} \sqrt{x-1} \, dx$ using a left Riemann sum with 4 subintervals.
Use a right Riemann sum with $n = 6$ to estimate $\int_{0}^{3} \frac{1}{x+2} \, dx$.

Given the table, use a right Riemann sum with 3 subintervals to estimate $\int_{0}^{15} h(t) \, dt$.

$\begin{array}{c|c|c|c|c} t & 0 & 5 & 10 & 15 \\ \hline h(t) & 12 & 8 & 10 & 6 \end{array}$
Approximate the area under $f(x) = x^2 - 2x + 2$ on $[-1,2]$ using a midpoint Riemann sum with 3 subintervals.
The rate of water flow (L/s) is $r(t) = t\sin t$. Use a right Riemann sum with 4 subintervals to estimate the total flow from $t = 0$ to $t = 4$.
questions.explanation: $\Delta t = 1$; right endpoints: $t = 1, 2, 3, 4$. $r(1) \approx 0.8415$, $r(2) \approx 1.8186$, $r(3) \approx 0.4234$, $r(4) \approx -3.027$. Sum $\approx 0.0565$, times 1 = 0.0565 L.

questions.question: For $f(x) = \frac{1}{\sqrt{x}}$ on $[1,9]$, use a left Riemann sum with 4 subintervals.

Given the table with unequal spacing, use a right Riemann sum to approximate $\int_{0}^{5} f(x) \, dx$.

$\begin{array}{c|c|c|c|c|c} x & 0 & 1 & 2 & 4 & 5 \\ \hline f(x) & 2 & 3 & 5 & 4 & 1 \end{array}$
questions.explanation: Subintervals: [0,1], [1,2], [2,4], [4,5] with widths 1, 1, 2, 1. Right endpoints: $f(1) = 3$, $f(2) = 5$, $f(4) = 4$, $f(5) = 1$. Sum = $3\times1 + 5\times1 + 4\times2 + 1\times1 = 17$.

questions.question: Approximate $\int_{0}^{\pi} \sin x \, dx$ using a midpoint Riemann sum with $n = 6$.
A velocity function is $v(t) = 20 - 2t$. Use a midpoint sum with 5 subintervals to estimate displacement from $t = 0$ to $t = 5$.
Which of the following represents a left Riemann sum with $n = 8$ to approximate $\int_{0}^{2} e^{-x^2} \, dx$?
questions.explanation: $\Delta x = 0.25$; left endpoints: $0, 0.25, 0.5, \dots, 1.75$. General form: $0.25 \sum_{k=0}^{7} e^{-(0.25k)^2}$.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You have mastered Riemann sum approximations.
scoreMessages.70: Good job! You understand the basics of Riemann sums. Review midpoint calculations.
scoreMessages.50: Keep practicing! Focus on identifying endpoints and calculating $\Delta x$ correctly.
scoreMessages.0: Review the definition of Riemann sums: $\sum f(x_i^*) \Delta x$.
